source_jobs.title: Senior Fleet Underwriter
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR UNDERWRITER


WHO WE ARE

Nirvana is on a mission to harness the power of data to revolutionize commercial insurance and enable a safer world. We are bringing much-needed innovation into the legacy, trillion-dollar commercial insurance industry. We have developed cutting-edge predictive models that use real-time IoT data from billions of connected devices, allowing us to better understand and price risk. Our AI-driven platform fundamentally changes the way an insurance company operates with personalized risk scoring, faster underwriting, modernized claims, and proactive, data-driven insights to help customers prevent accidents.

We’ve already proven the scale—serving thousands of fleets and more than doubling our written premiums year over year. Our data moat is growing exponentially with more than 30 billion miles of telematics data, leading to more predictive models and new insights into how we can better understand and reduce risk. Altogether, our loss ratio, efficiency, and customer experience are redefining what can be done in the industry.

With $270+ million raised, including our Series D round in December 2025, we’re only accelerating our growth, with strong support from top-tier VCs including Lightspeed, General Catalyst, and Valor. Nirvana’s leadership team has previously helped scale multi-billion-dollar companies from scratch, including Samsara, Rubrik, and Flexport, and includes industry veterans from Hiscox, The Hartford, and RLI.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Fleet Underwriter who embodies what next-generation underwriting looks like at Nirvana: less time processing files, more time making decisions, building relationships, and closing business. As our AI platform absorbs the initial exposure validation, pricing prep, and file review, you’ll step in where it matters most—making the call, leading the negotiation, and owning the broker relationship. You’ll also play an active role in making our models smarter, surfacing what the AI misses and feeding that context back to our Product team. This is a high-visibility role at the intersection of technical expertise, market relationships, and executive presence.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Review AI-generated submission analyses, pricing outputs, and exposure summaries—treating every output as a first draft and identifying what the model missed before making a decision.

 - Make fast, precise underwriting decisions on commercial trucking accounts, knowing when to approve, modify, decline, or escalate.

 - Catch inconsistencies in submission data that AI may miss: contradictions across operation class, mileage, commodity, FMCSA safety scores, DOT history, and loss run patterns.

 - Read and explain all pricing components—tier, experience mod, territory factor, schedule mod—and build a rate rationale you can walk a broker through without reconstructing the math.

 - Track carrier appetite, competitive positioning, and where Nirvana wins and where it doesn’t; when a broker asks “where does this land?” you answer from experience, not a guess.

 - Build and deepen broker relationships proactively. Be the underwriter brokers call with their best accounts because they trust you to respond quickly, price fairly, and not waste their time.

 - Lead deal negotiations with a clear floor, a story, and a position. Understand the broker’s situation and competitive set—know when to move and when to hold.

 - Present coverage, pricing, and Nirvana’s value proposition to insured leadership teams—including CFOs and fleet managers—with confidence and clarity on large accounts.

 - Document model blind spots with specific account-tied context and share structured feedback with Product to improve AI accuracy over time. This is part of the job, not optional.

 - Mentor junior underwriters on risk identification, pricing fluency, broker engagement, and how to work effectively alongside AI tools.




WHAT YOU BRING

Must-Haves

 - 5+ years of commercial trucking underwriting

source_jobs.title: Sr Manager, Corporate Development
source_jobs.description:
Who we are:

Nirvana is on a mission to harness the power of data to revolutionize commercial insurance and enable a safer world. We are bringing much-needed innovation into the legacy, trillion-dollar commercial insurance industry. We have developed cutting-edge predictive models that use real-time IoT data from billions of connected devices, allowing us to better understand and price risk. Our AI-driven platform fundamentally changes the way an insurance company operates with personalized risk scoring, faster underwriting, modernized claims, and proactive, data-driven insights to help customers prevent accidents.



We've already proven the scale—serving thousands of fleets and more than doubling our written premiums year over year. Our data moat is growing exponentially with more than 30 billion miles of telematics data, leading to more predictive models and new insights into how we can better understand and reduce risk.



With $270+ million raised, including our Series D round in December 2025, we're only accelerating our growth, with strong support from top-tier VCs including Lightspeed, General Catalyst, and Valor. Nirvana's leadership team has previously helped scale multi-billion-dollar companies from scratch, including Samsara, Rubrik, and Flexport, and includes industry veterans from Hiscox, The Hartford, and RLI.



About the role:

Reporting directly to senior leadership, you'll be Nirvana's first dedicated Corporate Development hire—building our M&A and strategic partnerships function from the ground up. You'll work closely with Strategic Finance, Claims leadership, and our data partnerships team to identify, evaluate, and execute transactions that accelerate Nirvana's growth across both our insurance MGA and claims TPA businesses. This is a high-visibility, high-autonomy role for someone who wants to shape strategy at a fast-growing InsureTech company, not just execute on a predefined playbook.



What you'll do:

 - Lead full-lifecycle M&A transactions from investment thesis development through sourcing, diligence, negotiation, deal structuring, legal documentation, and closing for both insurance and claims acquisitions

 - Build and manage direct and intermediary relationships to source proprietary deal flow and stay ahead of market opportunities in the MGA and claims sectors

 - Identify, structure, and launch strategic partnerships, particularly around data acquisition opportunities that strengthen Nirvana's underwriting and risk models

 - Scope, negotiate, and launch large commercial deals involving data assets and other strategic resources that support Nirvana's competitive moat



About you:

 - 4–7 years of experience in private equity, investment banking, or corporate development

 - Insurance or InsureTech experience strongly preferred; FinTech background acceptable

 - Demonstrated experience managing deals end-to-end, including diligence, negotiation, and closing

 - Comfortable operating as an independent contributor with minimal oversight

 - Strong financial modeling and valuation skills

 - Experience managing intermediary relationships (investment bankers, advisors)

 - Strong communication skills, both written & verbal

 - Thorough critical thinker who isn't afraid to make decisions

 - Self-starter who independently seeks out opportunities to learn & succeed

 - Bias-to-action — not afraid to get your hands dirty and stretch yourself

 - Owner mentality — takes accountability for actions and results

 - Thrives in high-growth, ambiguous startup environments



Benefits:

- Competitive salary & equity

- Medical, dental & vision insurance (100% employer paid)

- 401k with up to 4% company match

- Unlimited PTO

- Parental leave

- Wellness reimbursement



We set our salary ranges using compensation data from companies similar to our stage and size. For this role, the estimated total cash for this position is $232,000- $256,000. The actual total cash salary will vary depending on multiple fac

source_jobs.title: Compliance Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Who we are:
Nirvana is on a mission to harness the power of data to revolutionize commercial insurance and enable a safer world. We are bringing much-needed innovation into the legacy, trillion-dollar commercial insurance industry. We have developed cutting-edge predictive models that use real-time IoT data from billions of connected devices, allowing us to better understand and price risk. Our AI-driven platform fundamentally changes the way an insurance company operates with personalized risk scoring, faster underwriting, modernized claims, and proactive, data-driven insights to help customers prevent accidents.

We’ve already proven the scale—serving thousands of fleets and more than doubling our written premiums year over year. Our data moat is growing exponentially with more than 30 billion miles of telematics data, leading to more predictive models and new insights into how we can better understand and reduce risk. Altogether, our loss ratio, efficiency, and customer experience are redefining what can be done in the industry.

With $270+ million raised, including our Series D round in December 2025, we’re only accelerating our growth, with strong support from top-tier VCs including Lightspeed, General Catalyst, and Valor. Nirvana’s leadership team has previously helped scale multi-billion-dollar companies from scratch, including Samsara, Rubrik, and Flexport, and includes industry veterans from Hiscox, The Hartford, and RLI.

About the role:
The Compliance Analyst will serve as a key member of the Compliance team, supporting day-to-day regulatory compliance operations across the organization's MGA and program business activities. Reporting to the Senior Manager of Compliance, this role is responsible for producer and adjuster licensing, regulatory correspondence, state filings assistance, legislative monitoring, and related compliance program functions. The ideal candidate brings solid foundational insurance regulatory experience, a detail-oriented work style, and the intellectual curiosity to navigate complex, multi-state compliance requirements in a fast-paced environment. Equally important is a genuine openness — or hands-on experience — with AI tools and technology; our compliance function is highly AI-driven, and we are looking for someone who is enthusiastic about leveraging AI to work smarter and more efficiently.

What you’ll do:
Regulatory Engagement & Response

 - Draft, review, and coordinate responses to state insurance department inquiries, market conduct examination requests, and information requests.

 - Manage complaint handling workflows, including tracking, investigation support, and timely response submissions to regulators through NAIC complaint portals and direct state channels.

 - Support the state filings manager with research requests related to objection responses to rate, rule, and form filings, including review of applicable statutes, regulations, and department guidance.

 - Maintain organized files and audit trails for all regulatory correspondence.


Producer & Adjuster Licensing

 - Administer the company's producer licensing program, including new applications, renewals, continuing education tracking, and bond renewals across all active states.

 - Monitor and ensure compliance with adjuster licensing requirements in applicable jurisdictions, including independent adjuster entity and individual licensing obligations.

 - Serve as primary liaison with third-party licensing vendors and state departments of insurance on licensing matters.

 - Maintain accurate and current subproducer licensing records in the compliance management system.

Legislative & Regulatory Monitoring

 - Monitor state and federal legislative, regulatory, and appellate court developments affecting commercial lines insurance, surplus lines, and motor carrier programs.

 - Summarize and communicate relevant changes to internal stakeholders, including underwriting, operations, and leadership.

 - Maintain a regulatory tra

notes: Nirvana is an insurance company with a focus on data-driven innovation, but the job postings do not indicate a strong engineering core.
